changes.value:
swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/install/components/mod.rs:1244-1246
avp-cli/src/install.rs:26-31

The new `AvpHooks` component declares `is_applicable` returning `false` for `InitScope::User`, meaning `sah init --user` (or whatever the global flag maps to) will skip AVP hook installation with status "skipped".

However, the existing `avp-cli` `install` function (which is the thin wrapper in avp-cli/src/install.rs) still supports `InstallTarget::User` by mapping it to `InitScope::User` and calling `avp_common::install::install`. So the two entry points (`avp init` and `sah init`) now have divergent behavior for the User scope: `avp init --user` installs AVP hooks globally, but `sah init --user` silently skips them.

This asymmetry was presumably intentional (maybe user-scope hooks are managed differently in sah), but it is undocumented. If it is intentional, add a doc comment to `is_applicable` explaining why User scope is excluded. If it is not intentional, remove the `is_applicable` override.

Verification: Confirm intended behavior by checking how `sah init --user` / `sah init --global` is invoked and what scope it maps to. Add a test or comment documenting the decision."
warning: AvpHooks::is_applicable excludes User scope but avp-cli install still supports it
